title: Secure credentialed expert reviewer(s): WASH/EH + clinician
context: No HIGH-risk content (M2: ORS/dehydration, chemical dosing) may begin until a credentialed expert reviewer is secured. The credential bar is specific: a WASH/environmental-health professional (MPH in environmental health, registered sanitarian/REHS, or equivalent) for water-treatment/dosing content, and a licensed clinician (physician or nurse) for ORS/dehydration danger-sign content. Outreach deadline is 2026-09-30; if unmet, M2 does not start. This task documents outreach, engagement, consent, and the reviewers ledger entry.
objective: Identify, outreach to, and secure commitment from a credentialed WASH/environmental-health professional (for dosing/water-treatment content) and a licensed clinician (for ORS/dehydration content) to serve as expert reviewers, with credentials and consent recorded in the reviewers ledger; establish version-scoped sign-off and veto/escalation rules.
acceptanceCriteria: A WASH/environmental-health professional (e.g., MPH in environmental health, registered sanitarian, or field-recognized WASH/EH equivalent) agrees to review and sign off dosing/water-treatment HIGH-risk units, with their credential, affiliation, and consent to be credited recorded in the reviewers ledger
A licensed clinician (physician or nurse) agrees to review and sign off ORS/dehydration danger-sign HIGH-risk units, with their credential, affiliation, and consent recorded in the reviewers ledger

Version-scoped sign-off rules are documented: sign-off attaches to a specific content version and citation/numeric set; edits require re-sign-off; expert veto on whether a HIGH-risk unit is safe to ship stands and cannot be overridden by a maintainer
Veto/escalation rules are documented: disagreement between expert and maintainer is logged and escalated to a second expert or Elyos governance
Reviewers ledger entry committed to the repo (with credential, consent, and scope fields); outreach date and agreement date recorded
On successful completion, HIGH-risk content tasks (content-015, content-016, content-017) are unblocked
output: A committed reviewers ledger document recording the secured credentialed expert reviewer(s) with credentials, consent, and version-scoped sign-off and veto rules; outreach and agreement dates recorded.
